Located in the Golden Gate Park near 2 museums and 2 other gardens.  Recommend getting a garden pass online for all day entry to 3 gardens.  The garden is well marked with various areas known for their beautiful trees and flowers.  Be sure to see the large Sequoia trees.  Take your time to see the beauty and well-done design.  Highly recommend.  Staff is very helpful.

A lovely garden of native Californian and climate-analog plants. Formerly known as Strybing Arboretum and once free to all, they began charging out-of-towners about 20 years ago. Admission is now up to $15 per adult. The value depends on how you plan to engage with the garden. For a landscape designer doing research it's fine. But it's hard to justify $30 for a couple to spend an hour strolling when the rest of GGP is right there. Free windows exist before 9am and first Tuesdays, and SF residents enter at no cost.

Absolutely stunning, magical and calm place to reconnect with nature. We went during spring so Magnolias were in bloom- and everything else! Paths are mostly paved so definitely stroller friendly. You can take advantage as some days are free before 9am. Also included in Museums for All if you have an EBT or WIC card from any state.
